description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a heavy, wedge-shaped metal head. The handle appears worn and smooth, indicating frequent use, and is ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip. The axe head is forged from dark, weathered metal, with a broad, flat poll and a single, sharp cutting edge. The blade is thick and utilitarian, lacking decorative elements, and is securely mounted onto the handle. The overall design is practical, suitable for chopping wood or demolition tasks in urban environments such as parks, garages, or construction sites. No visible branding or markings are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a worn wooden handle and a heavy, wedge-shaped metal head. The design is utilitarian and practical, with a single sharp edge and no visible markings.
description_view_0: The axe is seen at an angle, highlighting the length and curvature of the wooden handle and the thickness of the metal head.
description_view_1: A side profile view shows the full width of the axe head and the straight alignment of the handle beneath it.
description_view_2: This view emphasizes the tapering handle and the angled, sharp edge of the blade.
description_view_3: A direct side view displays the broad face of the axe head and the full length of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, smooth and worn, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a broad poll and no beard, utilitarian profile.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting, blade fitted onto handle with visible swelling at the top.
